subject: Sociology
title: Urban Education Challenges
description: A quick, practical guide to Urban Education Challenges.

hook.text: Two schools sit five miles apart. One has a theater and lab. The other lacks updated textbooks.

buildup.text: Urban schools in poor districts face funding gaps tied to local property taxes — poor areas get less money.

discovery.text: Teacher turnover, overcrowding, and safety concerns compound the disadvantage in under-resourced urban schools.

twist.text: Charter schools and choice programs were meant to help but often drain resources from public schools further.

climax.text: Urban education inequality reproduces class divisions. Schools meant to equalize end up reinforcing the gap.

punchline.text: School quality follows the tax base.

quiz.question: Why do urban schools in poor areas often lack resources?
options: Funding is tied to local property taxes
